entry.title: Search-R1+: Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning for Search-Augmented Reasoning Agents
entry.summary: We present Search-R1+, a tool-use reinforcement learning framework that trains reasoning agents to interleave search calls with chain-of-thought. The method combines a policy gradient objective with retrieval-augmented verification and demonstrates strong gains on multi-hop QA, web research, and long-form reasoning benchmarks. Code and checkpoints are released.

entry.title: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Pure Computer Vision Object Detection
entry.summary: We present an exhaustive benchmark for object detection in the wild, covering 50 datasets and 30 baselines. Our analysis focuses on leaderboard performance, with ablations on model size and training schedules. We do not propose a new method.

entry.title: Paper Reading Agents with Long-Context Retrieval and Self-Critique
entry.summary: We introduce a paper reading agent that combines long-context retrieval and a self-critic loop to generate research-grade briefs from PDFs. The agent improves over single-pass summarization on a curated benchmark of 200 ML papers, with strong reproducibility signals: code, prompts, and evaluation harness are released.

entry.title: LoRA Training at Scale: Empirical Study on Long-Context Adaptation
entry.summary: We empirically study low-rank adaptation for long-context language models up to 256k tokens, revealing trade-offs between rank, memory, and downstream performance. Workflow automation tooling and training recipes are released.
